author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Picked option 1 (declare a cap): 65,536 bytes raw UTF-8, matching
GitHub's documented issue-body limit. New `BODY_MAX_BYTES` constant
+ `validate_body` helper in jjf-storage; rejected via typed
`Error::InvalidBody { reason: BodyInvalidReason::TooLong }`. Wired
in at every body entry point: `create_issue` (seed body),
`set_body`, `update` (body field), AND `add_comment` (same shape /
on-disk surface / risk as the issue body — opted not to scope this
out). CLI pre-validates in `run_new`, `run_update`, `run_comment`
so the operator sees the typed exit-2 envelope before any IO.

JSON envelope shape (exit 2, preflight):
`{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"body_too_large","message":"...","details":{"limit":65536,"got":<N>}}}`.
`limit` + `got` are flat JSON integers in `details` (not strings,
not nested). Pre-fix C3 of the QA harness reported `info: 10MB
body exit=0`; post-fix it reports `NEGATIVE: 10MB body rejected at
preflight (exit 2)`. Spec amended at `docs/storage-format.md` §3
(issue body) and §4 (comment body); `docs/cli-json.md` error
table gained a `body_too_large` row, a dedicated note section,
and an example envelope.

## Recommendation

Closed. Cap matches the prior-art number every integration
already expects (GitHub/Forgejo `mediumblob`-backed surface
documented as 65,536 chars; we measure bytes for ASCII-equivalence
and unambiguous multi-byte handling). Tests: 8 storage-level
(boundary at 65,536, reject at +1, multi-byte measurement, each
of create/set/update/add-comment), 4 CLI integration (new / new
at-cap / update / comment). All 532 workspace tests green.

## Confidence

High. The boundary is byte-exact (tested at 65,536 = accept,
65,537 = reject, including a multi-byte body where the byte-cap
fires before the scalar-cap would). The rejection is wired at
both layers (CLI preflight + storage re-validation), so a
programmatic caller can't bypass the cap.

## Open follow-ups

- The 10MB-title arm of C3 still reports
  `FINDING-CANDIDATE: 10MB title exit 126 — check stderr`. Exit
  126 means the inline `bash -c` got `Argument list too long`
  before jjf ever ran (POSIX ARG_MAX, not a jjforge contract
  gap). Not in scope for this ticket; file separately if QA
  wants `--title-file` for parity with `-F`.
- Document body-cap behavior in any future `jjf` man page /
  README once those exist (today the contract lives in the spec
  files this ticket amended).
